summary: Lamentations mourns the destruction of Jerusalem and the suffering of the people while affirming that God’s mercy remains even in catastrophe.
historical_context: Traditionally attributed to Jeremiah, the book reflects the devastation following the Babylonian conquest of Jerusalem in 586 BCE.

mirror_water_fire.mirror: The destruction of Jerusalem reflects the cost of national disobedience.
mirror_water_fire.water: Lament purifies grief by bringing suffering before God.
mirror_water_fire.fire: Hope emerges from sorrow through trust in divine compassion.
